ksa_text: Conduct evaluations of data and AI tools and platforms, including requirements definition, vendor engagement, proof-of-concept design and execution, technical and commercial assessment, testing and compliance evaluation, and selection recommendation.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of intelligence requirements tasking systems and software

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: This task KSA describes execution-level work in Operations & Program Management that is not expected of a Data and AI Transformation Lead (senior specialist tier). The role is responsible for overseeing and directing this work, not performing it directly. Excluded on execution-seniority grounds.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this execution-level task KSA be expected of a Data and AI Transformation Lead in real-world practice?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Compensation benchmarks and leadership effectiveness research confirm that senior specialist-tier roles direct and oversee execution work in Operations & Program Management rather than performing it. Role scope analysis supports exclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: execution-level task KSA in Operations & Program Management is appropriately excluded for a non-execution senior specialist role. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Execution-level task KSA in Operations & Program Management is outside the expected competency scope for a Data and AI Transformation Lead (senior specialist tier). The role oversees this work; it does not perform it directly.
